worth_it_explanation: Display and Video 360 (DV360) line items default to targeting all public inventory, which means spend reaches every exchange a line item has access to unless the practitioner actively narrows it. These checks are fast to run in the DV360 interface. Each one targets a setting that exists at the line item or insertion order (IO) level, and a misconfiguration here affects every impression the line item buys. The fixes are configuration changes, not rebuilds, and they take effect on the next auction the line item enters.
intro: In DV360, inventory source targeting controls where a line item's budget can go. Each line item can buy from open auction across one or more exchanges, from private marketplace (PMP) deals, from inventory packages, or from some combination. DV360 defaults to open auction targeting across all available exchanges unless the practitioner restricts it. These checks confirm that inventory source targeting reflects deliberate choices rather than defaults, that private deals are actually in use when they appear in the account, and that open auction buying is accompanied by the brand safety and quality controls that public inventory requires.

audit_checks.title: Line items target all public inventory rather than a defined set of exchanges or deals
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A line item set to all public inventory buys from every exchange it has access to without regard for quality, relevance, or brand safety. The budget distributes across the widest possible supply pool, including exchanges with higher proportions of low-quality or non-viewable inventory, until a separate exclusion layer or brand safety setting reins it in.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 (DV360) assigns each line item a default inventory source setting of 'Public inventory', which targets all exchanges the advertiser's partner has access to. Nothing in the DV360 new-line-item workflow prompts a practitioner to narrow that setting. The result is that many line items run on every available exchange unless someone explicitly restricts the targeting.

Open auction across all exchanges is not inherently wrong, but it is a deliberate choice, not a neutral default. Different exchanges carry meaningfully different inventory quality profiles. Some specialize in premium video or direct publisher relationships. Others aggregate inventory from a wide range of resellers, which introduces variability in viewability, brand safety, and invalid traffic (IVT) rates. Treating them all identically means the line item's quality outcome depends entirely on the downstream brand safety and exclusion settings rather than on the upstream supply selection.

A more intentional approach is to restrict targeting to the exchanges that match the campaign's goals. A connected TV (CTV) campaign has no reason to target mobile web exchanges. A direct-response display campaign can be scoped to the exchanges where the advertiser's first-party data shows conversion activity. A private marketplace (PMP) deal campaign may not need open auction at all.

A few patterns suggest inventory source targeting has not been reviewed:
  • Line items running on display, video, and CTV formats all carry identical public inventory settings rather than exchange-specific targeting
  • Line items with private deals also have open auction targeting switched on, so deal spend and open auction spend compete from the same budget
  • No exchange has been restricted despite the campaign running for multiple weeks
You may want to review the exchange-level performance breakdown in DV360 reporting before restricting targeting. Removing a well-performing exchange to tighten the supply list could reduce delivery, so the adjustment is best made with data behind it.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, display and video line items targeting product audiences or past-purchaser lists are typically most efficient on exchanges where those audiences deliver high match rates. You may want to review the exchange-level conversion report and consider scoping open auction targeting to the exchanges where the audience matches and purchases concentrate.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, the conversion event is a form submission, which requires a viewable, non-fraudulent impression on a page where the user is genuinely present. You may want to consider restricting open auction targeting to exchanges with lower IVT rates and higher average viewability for the formats the campaign uses.

learn_more.source_title: Target inventory sources in Display and Video 360

investigate.intro: Review the inventory source targeting on each active line item and confirm whether the exchange selection reflects a deliberate choice.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the insertion order (IO) and navigate to the line item list. Select a line item, open 'Targeting', and find the 'Inventory source' section.
Note whether the line item targets 'Public inventory' across all exchanges or a defined subset of exchanges. Note any private deals or inventory packages alongside open auction.

For each line item where open auction across all exchanges is on, open the line item's performance report filtered by exchange. Identify which exchanges are delivering impressions, which are delivering conversions or measurable outcomes, and which exchanges are delivering a high share of impressions with few or no outcomes.
Repeat for the remaining active line items in the IO. Note any line item where the exchange distribution shows significant delivery on exchanges with no measurable outcome for the campaign goal.

evaluate.logic: For each active line item, record the inventory source setting (all public, restricted exchanges, deals only, or a combination) and whether the setting reflects a documented rationale. A line item with exchange targeting restricted to a defined set, or with open auction limited to exchanges showing measurable performance, passes. A line item with all-exchange open auction enabled and no exchange-level performance review on record is a fail, recorded with the line item name and the exchange distribution finding.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each active line item's inventory source setting and the exchange-level performance observation to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that active line items in {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 (DV360) account target all public inventory by default rather than a defined set of exchanges. The budget distributes across every exchange the partner has access to without an exchange-level quality or performance filter. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the exchange-level performance breakdown for each active line item and restricting open auction targeting to the exchanges where the campaign's audience and conversion activity concentrate. For line items running alongside private marketplace (PMP) deals, {{voice_recommend}} confirming whether open auction targeting alongside the deal budget is intentional or whether the deal was meant to be the primary supply path.

audit_checks.title: Line items buy a significant share of impressions through resellers rather than authorized direct sellers
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Impressions bought through long reseller chains carry higher invalid traffic (IVT) risk and lower price efficiency because each intermediary adds fees before the bid reaches the publisher. Supply path optimization (SPO) in DV360 allows buyers to prefer direct or shorter paths, but only if the practitioner enables it.

educate.base:
When a DV360 line item buys from open auction, the bid travels through a supply chain before reaching the publisher. A direct path means the exchange that serves the bid is authorized by the publisher to sell that publisher's inventory, and the publisher appears as a direct seller in that exchange's ads.txt file. A reseller path means the impression passes through one or more intermediaries between the exchange and the publisher, each of which may add fees, reduce transparency, and introduce a layer where IVT is harder to detect.

The Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) ads.txt standard was designed to address this. Publishers list the exchanges and resellers authorized to sell their inventory in a publicly readable ads.txt file. A buyer whose exchange matches a direct-seller entry in the publisher's ads.txt file is buying through a direct, authorized path. A buyer whose exchange matches only a reseller entry, or does not appear in the ads.txt file at all, is buying through a longer chain.

DV360 surfaces supply path information in its inventory management tools and supports SPO settings at the partner level. SPO in DV360 can prefer direct sellers over resellers when both paths are available for the same impression. Without this setting, DV360 bids across all paths based on price and delivery, which means the cheapest path wins regardless of path length.

A few signs point to reseller-heavy supply paths in an account:
  • The partner's SPO settings show no preference for direct sellers or authorized resellers
  • A meaningful share of display or video spend lands on inventory that exchange-level reporting cannot match to a confirmed publisher domain
  • Third-party verification data shows a higher than expected proportion of impressions with no detected publisher domain
You may want to confirm the partner-level SPO setting before adjusting line item targeting. The SPO preference affects all line items under the partner, so the change is most efficient at that level.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser running prospecting campaigns, a high reseller share means a portion of the brand exposure budget is reaching impressions that are harder to verify as legitimate publisher environments. You may want to review whether the exchanges used for prospecting have strong direct-seller relationships in the verticals the campaign targets.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, form submission events require a real user on a real page, and reseller-heavy paths are statistically more likely to include non-human traffic. You may want to consider whether the cost-per-lead on exchanges with high reseller shares compares favorably to exchanges with predominantly direct paths after accounting for IVT filtering.

learn_more.source_title: Target inventory sources in Display and Video 360
About supply path optimization in Display and Video 360

investigate.intro: Review the partner-level supply path optimization (SPO) setting and confirm whether the account prefers direct seller paths for open auction buying.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the partner settings in DV360 and navigate to the 'Exchange settings' or 'Inventory' section. Look for the supply path optimization setting and note whether it is on and configured to prefer direct or authorized sellers.

In the Inventory module, open the 'My inventory' section and review the deals and exchange relationships active for the partner. Note whether any exchanges show a high share of reseller-sourced inventory in their descriptions or labels.

Run a line item performance report broken down by exchange. For exchanges with significant spend, note whether the inventory source type label in the report describes the seller path as direct or as reseller where that data is available.

If a third-party verification vendor such as DoubleVerify or Integral Ad Science (IAS) is active, pull the supply chain or IVT report and review the share of impressions flagged as coming through non-direct paths.

doubleverify_ias.label: DoubleVerify or IAS (if active)
steps: Open the verification vendor's dashboard and filter to the DV360 campaigns under review.
Look for a supply chain or inventory quality report. Note the share of impressions flagged as non-direct, reseller-only, or path-unverified.
Cross-reference the exchanges with the highest reseller share against the line items that delivered the most impressions on those exchanges.

evaluate.logic: Record the partner-level SPO setting (on with direct seller preference, on with no preference, or off) and any verification vendor finding on reseller share. A partner with SPO set to prefer direct sellers and no significant reseller-flag volume from a verification vendor passes. A partner with SPO off or set to no preference, or with a verification vendor showing a high reseller share, is a fail recorded with the SPO setting and the reseller-share figure.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the partner SPO setting and any verification vendor reseller-share data to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 (DV360) partner does not have supply path optimization (SPO) configured to prefer direct sellers, meaning open auction bids travel through reseller chains rather than prioritizing direct publisher relationships. Reseller-heavy supply paths introduce additional fees at each intermediary and are statistically associated with higher invalid traffic (IVT) rates. {{voice_recommend}} enabling SPO at the partner level with a preference for direct and authorized sellers, and confirming the setting with the exchanges that carry the highest impression share for the account's active line items.

audit_checks.title: Open auction buying is active without the brand safety and quality exclusions it requires
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Open auction gives a line item access to the broadest possible supply pool, including inventory that carries brand safety risk, high IVT rates, and low viewability. Without a configured exclusion layer, the line item bids on every impression the exchange offers, and the brand pays for adjacencies and environments it would not knowingly choose.

educate.base:
Open auction in Display and Video 360 (DV360) makes a line item eligible for any impression available on the exchanges it targets. The breadth of that supply pool is the feature's purpose, but it means the line item will also bid on content that a brand would not choose to appear next to if it reviewed each placement individually. Without active controls, DV360 does not apply brand safety filtering beyond whatever the exchange itself enforces at its own threshold, which varies by exchange.

DV360 provides several layers of quality control that a practitioner can apply to open auction buying. Digital content labels (DL-G through DL-MA) filter by the audience appropriateness rating Google assigns to content. Sensitive category exclusions block inventory in specific topics such as tragedy and conflict, politically sensitive content, or gambling. Publisher and app blocklists exclude specific domains or app IDs. Third-party verification integrations with vendors such as DoubleVerify or Integral Ad Science (IAS) can apply pre-bid filtering before the impression is ever purchased.

These controls work at different levels. Digital content labels and sensitive category exclusions apply at the advertiser or line item level inside DV360. Third-party pre-bid filtering applies through the verification vendor's integration, which must be activated and assigned to the line item. A line item with open auction targeting and none of these controls active is bidding on the full exchange supply pool at whatever the exchange's own minimum quality threshold is.

A few signs indicate open auction buying is running without adequate controls:
  • The line item's DV360 brand safety settings show no sensitive category exclusions and no digital content label restriction below 'DL-MA' (the least restrictive label)
  • No third-party verification pre-bid filter is assigned to the line item
  • Post-campaign reporting from a verification vendor shows a meaningful share of impressions in categories the brand would not have chosen
You may want to review the brand's own adjacency guidelines before choosing which exclusions to apply. The right set of exclusions reflects the brand's standards, not a generic list.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, a product ad appearing next to content in a sensitive or controversial category can create brand association the client did not intend and has no awareness of. You may want to confirm that at minimum the advertiser-level brand safety settings in DV360 include sensitive category exclusions appropriate for the product category and the target audience.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, the creative message is typically about a product, service, or offer, and brand context matters to the conversion intent the creative generates. You may want to confirm that sensitive category exclusions and a digital content label restriction are active on line items running open auction, particularly those targeting upper-funnel audiences who are in early consideration.

learn_more.source_title: Target inventory sources in Display and Video 360
About brand safety controls in Display and Video 360

investigate.intro: Confirm whether the advertiser-level and line item-level brand safety settings in DV360 include content label restrictions, sensitive category exclusions, and any third-party verification pre-bid filtering for line items running open auction.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the advertiser settings and navigate to 'Brand safety'. Note the digital content label setting. A setting of 'DL-MA' applies no label-based restriction. A setting of 'DL-G', 'DL-PG', or 'DL-T' restricts the supply to content Google has rated at or below that audience appropriateness level.
In the same brand safety settings, review the sensitive category exclusions. Note which categories are excluded and whether the exclusion list reflects the brand's adjacency guidelines.

Open each active line item running open auction and confirm whether its targeting inherits the advertiser-level brand safety settings or overrides them. A line item override that widens the advertiser settings is the highest-risk configuration.

Check whether a third-party verification vendor integration is active on the advertiser. If DoubleVerify or IAS is integrated, confirm whether a pre-bid filter is assigned to the line items in question.

doubleverify_ias.label: DoubleVerify or IAS (if active)
steps: Open the verification vendor's dashboard and pull the brand safety report for the DV360 campaigns under review.

Note the share of impressions that landed in categories covered by the brand's adjacency exclusions. Any meaningful share in an excluded or should-be-excluded category points to a gap in the pre-bid filter configuration.
Confirm whether the pre-bid filter assigned to the line items matches the categories in the brand's guidelines.

evaluate.logic: For each line item running open auction, record the digital content label setting, the active sensitive category exclusions, and whether a third-party pre-bid filter is assigned. A line item with a content label restriction appropriate for the brand, a sensitive category exclusion list aligned to the brand's guidelines, and either a third-party pre-bid filter or a documented rationale for not using one passes. A line item with no content label restriction, no sensitive category exclusions, and no pre-bid filter is a fail recorded with the line item name and the missing controls.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the content label setting, sensitive category exclusions, and pre-bid filter status for each open auction line item to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that line items in {{site_name}}'s DV360 account are running open auction buying without the brand safety controls that public inventory requires. The advertiser-level brand safety settings show no sensitive category exclusions and no digital content label restriction that limits the supply pool to content appropriate for the brand. Without these controls, the line items bid on the full range of exchange supply, including content categories the brand would not choose knowingly. {{voice_recommend}} configuring digital content label restrictions and sensitive category exclusions at the advertiser level based on the brand's adjacency guidelines, confirming that no active line items override those settings in a way that widens them, and evaluating whether a third-party verification pre-bid integration with a vendor such as DoubleVerify or Integral Ad Science (IAS) is appropriate for the campaigns.
